item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin is preparing to deliver a major address in St. Petersburg just days after the city was targeted by Ukrainian drones. It comes as Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is calling for a face-to-face meeting with Putin to end the years-long war. NBC’s Keir Simmons reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: Putin to Deliver Address Amid Pressure to End War in Ukraine
media:content.media:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin is preparing to deliver a major address in St. Petersburg just days after the city was targeted by Ukrainian drones. It comes as Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy is calling for a face-to-face meeting with Putin to end the years-long war. NBC’s Keir Simmons reports for TODAY.

item.title: Putin to Deliver Address Amid Pressure to End War in Ukraine

item.dc:creator: Steve Kopack
Pilar Melendez
Daniel Arkin
item.description: “60 Minutes” correspondents Leslie Stahl, Bill Whitaker and Jon Wertheim said Friday that they planned to stay on at the newsmagazine, capping days of turmoil.

media:content.media:text: Jon Wertheim, Bill Whitaker, Lesley Stahl.
media:content.media:description: “60 Minutes” correspondents Jon Wertheim, Bill Whitaker, Lesley Stahl.

item.title: Lesley Stahl, Bill Whitaker and Jon Wertheim say they’ll stay at ‘60 Minutes’

item.description: President Donald Trump scored a victory on Capitol Hill when Republicans in the Senate narrowly passed a bill to fund ICE and Border Patrol for the remainder of his term. Meanwhile, the president is unveiling a planned pedestrian bridge between the Lincoln Memorial and the Potomac River. NBC’s Garrett Haake reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: Senate Passes Bill to Fund ICE for Remainder of Trump's Term
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump scored a victory on Capitol Hill when Republicans in the Senate narrowly passed a bill to fund ICE and Border Patrol for the remainder of his term. Meanwhile, the president is unveiling a planned pedestrian bridge between the Lincoln Memorial and the Potomac River. NBC’s Garrett Haake reports for TODAY.

item.title: Senate Passes Bill to Fund ICE for Remainder of Trump's Term

item.description: Karen Read, along with her attorneys Alan Jackson and Charles Waters, exclusively join TODAY to speak out about their sweeping new lawsuit accusing Massachusetts State and Canton police of creating and tolerating a culture of bias and corruption, one year after a jury acquitted Read of the murder of her police officer boyfriend, John O’Keefe. “I had to fight for my freedom for years and I knew as it unfolded I was never going to be able to just forget that this happened to me, that I was wro

media:content.media:title: Karen Read Breaks Silence on Explosive Lawsuit Against Police
media:content.media:description: Karen Read, along with her attorneys Alan Jackson and Charles Waters, exclusively join TODAY to speak out about their sweeping new lawsuit accusing Massachusetts State and Canton police of creating and tolerating a culture of bias and corruption, one year after a jury acquitted Read of the murder of her police officer boyfriend, John O’Keefe. “I had to fight for my freedom for years and I knew as it unfolded I was never going to be able to just forget that this happened to me, that I was wronged in this way. I couldn’t just go back to life as it was. I have to continue fighting for justice,” Read says.

item.title: Karen Read Breaks Silence on Explosive Lawsuit Against Police

item.dc:creator: Allan Smith
item.description: Some anti-Trump Republicans have taken their political journey one step further: running for office as full-fledged Democrats. But so far in 2026, their new party’s primary voters haven’t shown much appetite for their candidacies.

media:content.media:description: George Conway in Milwaukee in 2024.
George Conway in Milwaukee in 2024.

item.title: Anti-Trump ex-Republicans are running in Democratic primaries. They’re off to a rocky start.

item.dc:creator: Natasha Korecki
Bridget Bowman
Sahil Kapur
item.description: For the Democratic Party, the road to Maine’s Senate primary is paved in dread.

media:content.media:description: Graham Platner, a Maine Democrat and U.S. Senate candidate, during an interview in York, Maine, on April 17, 2026.
Graham Platner greets supporters at an event hosted by Sen. Bernie Sanders in Orono, Maine, on May 24.
Maine Gov. Janet Mills.

item.title: Democrats navigate a new Graham Platner controversy in Maine’s battleground Senate race

item.dc:creator: Andrew Greif
item.description: Curaçao will make its World Cup debut this month as the smallest nation ever to qualify for the tournament, thanks to its connection to the Netherlands.

media:content.media:description: The Curaçao team huddles during a friendly match against Scotland in Glasgow on Saturday.
The Curaçao team huddles during a friendly match against Scotland in Glasgow on Saturday.
The Curaçao team huddles during a friendly match against Scotland in Glasgow on Saturday.

item.title: The smallest nation ever to qualify for the World Cup didn’t do it alone

item.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

media:content.media:title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
media:content.media:description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

item.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final

item.dc:creator: Gary Grumbach
Nathan Gonzalez
Corky Siemaszko
item.description: ALEXANDRIA, Va. — The former CIA officer who was found with $40 million in gold bars stashed at his Virginia home was in court Friday, where a judge ordered him held behind bars until he goes on trial for criminal theft of public money

media:content.media:text: The seal of Central Intelligence Agency in the lobby the headquarters building in Langley, Va., on Sept. 24, 2022.
media:content.media:description: The seal of Central Intelligence Agency in the lobby the headquarters building in Langley, Va., on Sept. 24, 2022.

item.title: Former CIA officer accused of stashing gold bars ordered to remain jailed pending trial

item.dc:creator: Tim Stelloh
item.description: The former IRS agent convicted in a deadly catfishing scheme targeting his wife and a stranger was sentenced Friday to a mandatory life term in prison

media:content.media:text: A tilted view of Brendan Banfield, seated in a courtroom.
media:content.media:description: Brendan Banfield in Fairfax County Circuit Court in Fairfax, Va., in January.

item.title: Brendan Banfield receives life sentence in elaborate au pair double murder plot

item.dc:creator: Pilar Melendez
item.description: Nearly a year after she was acquitted of murder charges in the death of her police officer boyfriend following two highly publicized trials, Karen Read said Friday that she still receives overwhelming support from legions of loyalists who believe she was framed.“No matter where I go — the grocery store, a restaurant, Newbury Street in Boston, any hotel or restaurant I eat at — I don’t pay for meals.

media:content.media:text: Karen Read interview on the TODAY Show on Jun. 5, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Karen Read interview on the TODAY Show on Jun. 5, 2026.

item.title: Karen Read says she doesn’t ‘pay for meals’ as support continues after acquittal

item.dc:creator: Tim Stelloh
Pilar Melendez
item.description: Karen Read, the Massachusetts woman who was acquitted of killing her police officer boyfriend last year after two widely publicized murder trials, revealed Friday why she filed a lawsuit alleging misconduct and negligence in the investigation that led to her prosecution.“This was always our plan, that I had to save my own life first,” Read said on “TODAY” while flanked by her two attorneys.

media:content.media:description: Karen Read arrives for opening statements at her retrial on April 22, 2025. Read was charged with second-degree murder in the 2022 death of John O'Keefe.
Karen Read and John O'Keefe.

item.title: Karen Read reveals decision behind new lawsuit against city and police

item.dc:creator: Andrew Greif
item.description: Curaçao will make its World Cup debut this month as the smallest nation ever to qualify for the tournament, thanks to its connection to the Netherlands.

media:content.media:description: The Curaçao team huddles during a friendly match against Scotland in Glasgow on Saturday.
The Curaçao team huddles during a friendly match against Scotland in Glasgow on Saturday.
The Curaçao team huddles during a friendly match against Scotland in Glasgow on Saturday.

item.title: The smallest nation ever to qualify for the World Cup didn’t do it alone

item.dc:creator: Natasha Korecki
item.description: Pritzker, who is widely viewed as having 2028 White House aspirations, is tapping into an issue seen as important to voters.

media:content.media:description: Illinois Gov. JB Pritzker can't unilaterally halt the program, but the executive branch does wield some control over processing applications, according to his office.

item.title: Illinois Gov. JB Pritzker to suspend tax breaks offered to data centers

item.dc:creator: Ryan J. Reilly
item.description: Trump’s DOJ could still pay Jan. 6 rioters even without ‘anti-weaponization fund’

media:content.media:description: The Justice Department building in Washington.
The Justice Department building in Washington.

item.title: DOJ could still pay Jan. 6 rioters even without ‘anti-weaponization’ fund

item.dc:creator: Frank Thorp V
Brennan Leach
Sahil Kapur
item.description: The Senate passed legislation early Friday to fund President Donald Trump’s immigration enforcement agencies after intense bipartisan backlash over a $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund threatened to derail the bill.

media:content.media:description: The Capitol on June 3, 2026.

item.title: Senate passes $70B immigration bill, without limits on ‘anti-weaponization’ fund

item.dc:creator: Scott Wong
Kyle Stewart
item.description: Rep. Tom Kean, R-N.J., has offered little explanation for his ongoing absence. His social media posts highlight how Capitol Hill staffers have come to cover for bosses who are MIA.

media:content.media:description: Rep. Tom Kean, R-N.J., last voted in the House on March 5.

item.title: Missing congressman and his staff paint a picture of business as usual

item.dc:creator: Steve Kopack
item.description: The May jobs report, set to be released Friday morning, is expected to show that hiring has remained steady despite growing inflation.

media:content.media:text: Floridians Seek Employment Opportunities At Job Fair In Sunrise
media:content.media:description: Job seekers attend the Mega JobNewsUSA South Florida Job Fair held in the Amerant Bank Arena on April 30, 2026 in Sunrise, Flo.

item.title: U.S. added 172,000 jobs in May, even as inflation squeezed consumers

item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin is set to deliver a speech at a flagship annual economic forum Friday, days after Ukrainian drone strikes rocked the host city St.

media:content.media:text: Russia's President Vladimir Putin at the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum in Saint Petersburg on June 5, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Russia's President Vladimir Putin at the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum in Saint Petersburg on June 5, 2026.
The opening ceremony Thursday for the International Economic Forum in St. Petersburg, Russia.
A thick plume of black smoke over the port of St. Petersburg, Russia, on Wednesday, following a Ukrainian drone attack.

item.title: Putin rules out Zelenskyy meeting, touts a new world order at ‘Russian Davos’

item.dc:creator: Zoe Malin

item.description: Target just announced its dates for Target Circle Week also called Target Circle Deal Days. The sale event will take place fromTuesday, June 23 to Friday, June 26.

media:content.media:text: Target Circle Week deals are available online, in the app and in stores.

item.dc:creator: Cory Fernandez
item.description: Le Creuset Dutch ovens, stainless steel pans, nonstick ceramic skillets and more are up to 40% off at the brand’s Summer Cookware Event.

media:content.media:text: Le Creuset’s cookware is known for its sturdiness and excellent heat retention.
media:content.media:description: Le Creuset’s cookware is known for its sturdiness and excellent heat retention.

item.title: Le Creuset Summer Sale: Save $170 on Dutch ovens and up to 40% on nonstick pans and more

item.dc:creator: Zoe Malin
item.description: Amazon Prime Day 2026 is happening from June 23 to 26, and only Prime members get access. Here’s what you need to know and what’s new about the sale this year.

media:content.media:text: Amazon Prime Day 2026 is happening for four days this June, giving members access to deals earlier than usual and for longer.
media:content.media:description: Amazon Prime Day 2026 is happening for four days this June, giving members access to deals earlier than usual and for longer.

media:content.media:text: Amazon Prime Day 2026 is happening for four days this June, giving members access to deals earlier than usual and for longer.

item.title: When is Amazon Prime Day 2026? The official dates, plus more about the 4-day sale
